one_time_description: Transfer of unused Health Canada funds (Fonds inutilisés - Santé Canada) to accumulated surplus
economic_notes: Hydro-Quebec agreement (2002) provides total funds of $24,904,142 over 50 years for community development; royalty of $398,136 received in 2021. Hydro-Quebec Atikamekw Nehirowisiw agreement (2015) provides $7,612,146 to the Council over 50 years; royalty of $189,367 received in 2021. Multiple commercial enterprises including forestry, sawmill, telecommunications, gas, and economic development partnerships.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2021. Combined financial statements prepared under Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards. Entity includes Fonds de fonctionnement, Fonds d'entreprise - Comité d'Habitation, Fonds d'entreprise - Collectif de crédit, Fonds de développement économique et communautaire, and Fiducie Mictikw Coria. 14 government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. Major one-time item: $3,684,084 transfer of unused Health Canada funds to accumulated surplus. New long-term debt of $9,474,000 from Autorité financière des Premières Nations (AFPN). Tangible capital assets total includes both Fonds d'exploitation ($47,926,660) and Fonds d'habitation ($3,503,280) = $51,429,940. Prior year audited by a different auditor. No remuneration schedule found in extracted text. Revenue breakdown: ISC $27,489,691; Other federal $4,741,627; Provincial $4,547,515; Other $1,748,666; Investment gains $13,910. Total government transfers estimated as ISC + Other federal + Provincial = $36,778,833; OSR = $38,541,409 - $36,778,833 = $1,762,576.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
